headline: A US government export-control directive forced Anthropic to disable its Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for all customers within three days of release.
body: The Fable 5 and Mythos 5 shutdown dominated the window, with a US export-control directive cited as the cause, reportedly over jailbreak and access concerns, and commentary extending the ban to EU jurisdictions. Model regulation broke through as the central theme, raising questions about whether frontier models stay public or move to government-only access. On the speed front, two separate releases crossed 1,000 tokens per second: Google's DiffusionGemma, which applies diffusion techniques to text and shifts inference from memory-bound to compute-bound, and Xiaomi's trillion-parameter MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed mixture-of-experts model. Vibe coding and developer productivity also surged as new entrants, alongside Google's agent-first Antigravity platform and a Gemma-powered teaching game.

cited_items.title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
